period_label: May 2026 · bands + hike + B4 builders reconciled to verified figures (10 Jun 2026) · refreshed 09 Jun 2026
data_coverage_note: Joinings: 217 (Base-Live). OTJ: 83/134 from Rocket Offers, V3 cross-check 192/244. CTC/CPH/Hike from JM cohort 178. Time to Fill, Avg TAT (open), Active Reqs, Female % now sourced live from Snowflake CSPL_HR_DB (HR_ATS_PUBLIC). Window auto-rolls daily — end = today (IST).

overview.hike_note: Reconciled to verified figure 18.03% (10 Jun 2026). Prior JM-cohort calc (6.81%) under-counted eligible joiners — TA to re-verify formula/coverage.
overview.ttf_note: Snowflake CSPL_HR_DB · 109 ATS-linked joiners with JOINING_DATE in window (26 Apr→04 May)
overview.tat_note: Avg TAT (open) = 73.6 days · per Rocket Open REQs (May 2026 official)
overview.female_note: Snowflake CSPL_HR_DB · 26 Female / 266 ATS-tagged joiners with declared gender
overview.td_note: Cars24 active employees (EMPLOYEE_STATUS='Active', incl. former CAPL now merged into CSPL). Founder-grade talent-density tiles.
overview._source_note: Joinings, OTJ, drops, recruiter productivity, source-of-hire = Recruitment sheets / P&C app (exclude internal movements). HC, attrition, bands, tenure, open reqs, TAT = Snowflake CSPL+CAPL_HR_DB (live). Snowflake is NOT used for joinings — its base includes movements which we do not count as joinings.

_sources.drops / drop_rate / otj: Rocket Offers CSV (Recruitment Metrics) · Joining Status != 'Joined' for offered candidates
_sources.exits_in_window: Snowflake · CSPL_HR_DB EMPLOYEES · DATE_OF_EXIT in window
_sources.attrition_30d / window / monthly_project: Snowflake · exits / active HC with avg_hc denominator (deck formula)
_sources.cph / cph_data_rows: Joining Master CSV · Actual_CPH for window joiners (Finance posts monthly — lag is normal)
_sources.hike: Joining Master CSV · Hike % for window joiners synced to JM
_sources.ttf / ttf_data_rows: Snowflake · CSPL_HR_DB.HR_ATS_PUBLIC.OFFER_LETTERS_DETAILS ⋈ REQUISITIONS · AVG(DOJ − START_DATE) for window joiners
_sources.female_pct / female_n: Snowflake · CSPL_HR_DB.HR_ATS_PUBLIC.APPLICANTS ⋈ OFFER_LETTERS_DETAILS · LOWER(GENDER) IN ('male','female') — HRMS GENDER is hashed for privacy
_sources.senior_b4_plus: Snowflake · CSPL_HR_DB EMPLOYEES · BAND IN ('B4','B5','B6','Builder') AND DOJ in window
_sources.productivity / active_recruiters: Recruitment Metrics CSV · joinings/recruiter · 24 active recruiters (May'26)
_sources.td_* (Talent Density tiles): Snowflake live · CSPL_HR_DB + CAPL_HR_DB EMPLOYEES · founder-grade builder density per Vikram's #1 lever
_sources.joinings (295): Recruitment sheet / P&C app · joinings EXCLUDE internal movements (Snowflake base over-counts as it includes movements)
_sources.yet_to_join / drops / otj: P&C Apps Script app + Recruitment sheet (May'26). Drops = past-DOJ no-shows; YTJ = future-dated accepted offers.

overview._source: Joinings/OTJ/drops from Recruitment sheets + P&C app (May'26, excl. movements). HC/attrition/tenure/builder-density LIVE from Snowflake CSPL+CAPL_HR_DB (09 Jun 2026).

functionTable._source: Attached P&C workbook · OTJ-by-Dept_Type tab (May-26). Offers/Joined/Drops/OTJ recomputed; HC & attrition columns carried from last Snowflake sync.

_sources_by_metric.joinings_by_incl_pending: Snowflake · DOJ in window (any status incl. Pending)

leadership._source: Recruitment Data · Joining Data · B4+ hires with DOJ strictly in May 2026 (June/July-dated joiners excluded — they have not joined yet). Only B4+ joiner in May'26 = Bharat Garg (B5, DOJ 14-May).

diversity._source: P&C Apps Script app · May'26 joiners · 28 Female / 295 joinings = 9.5%. By-function/band carried from workbook.

sourcing._source: Recruitment Data · Joining Data > Source RT column · May 2026 filter · all segments combined (Business + Support + T/P/BI/DS). Per May'26 reconciliation.
sourcing._carried_note: Overall source mix re-pulled from Source RT (May'26, all segments). The inbound/outbound by-function cards below are a separate operational view (window cohort) and are not part of the May'26 overall-mix reconciliation.

channels.definition: % of total May'26 joinings by channel. OUTBOUND = we reached out (Sourced / Consultant); INBOUND = candidate came to us (Applicant / Referral / Rehire / Campus / Careers Page). 0% = no joinings tagged to that channel this month.

sourcing.by_function_note: Inbound vs Outbound shown as share of TOTAL joinings (reconciled to verified figures, 10 Jun 2026). Business 26.44% in / 67.12% out · Support 1.02% / 3.73% · TP BI DS 0.34% / 1.36%.

channel_chart.note: Channel-wise share of 295 May'26 joinings · joiner counts are % × total (rounded). 0 = no joinings tagged to that channel this month.

function_channel_matrix.note: Joiner COUNT by department x source channel. 178 of 295 joinings (60.3%) have a channel tagged in the recruitment detail sheet; the remaining 117 have no channel recorded (shown as a separate line so the total reflects all 295). 'Direct' = recruiter self-sourced (largely LinkedIn).

consultant_joiners.note: All Consultant-sourced joinings this period, with hiring recruiter.

drops._source: P&C Apps Script app · May'26 · Drops = past-DOJ not-joined; Yet-to-Join = future-dated accepted offers.

ta._source: Open reqs (714) for May 2026 from Recruitment Data. Closed (295) + YTJ (105) + productivity (12.5 = joinings ÷ 24 active recruiters) from P&C app (May'26).

trends._source: Joinings: Recruitment sheet / P&C app 12-mo trend (EXCLUDES internal movements). Attrition: Snowflake CSPL exits ÷ active HC, monthly.

window_extras._source: by_day + by_entity from Snowflake CSPL_HR_DB (DOJ in window, status IN Active/InActive). top_recruiters from Rocket Offers CSV (Snowflake doesn't track recruiter).
window_extras._carried_note: Carried from last sync (18 May 2026) — attached workbook is recruitment/offer + employer-brand centric and does not refresh this metric.

metrics_not_computable_for_window.Variable CPH: Pending Finance close — Actual_CPH not yet posted in Joining Master for window cohort

tech.cost_note: May 2026 Tech & Product figures, combining all four tabs (Tech Data + Product Data + Offered-Tech + Offered-Product). Technology = 243 (joined roster 191 + Offered-Tech pipeline 52); Product = 76 (joined 72 + Offered-Product 4). Data & AI 54 carried from last Snowflake sync. The band-split charts below reflect the joined roster only (191 / 72). Open reqs 164 (52 New / 9 Replacement) from workbook requisitions tab — narrower than company-wide ATS view.
tech._source: Attached P&C workbook · Tech builder roster + Product Mgmt roster + Requisitions tab
tech.source: Attached P&C workbook · Tech builder roster + Product Mgmt roster + Requisitions tab
tech.window: May 2026 · joined + offered (4 tabs combined) · band split = joined roster
tech.formula: Technology / Product (May) = joined + offered across Tech Data + Product Data + Offered-Tech + Offered-Product tabs; Band split = COUNT by Band on joined roster; Open Roles = SUM(no_of_openings) on Status=Open reqs

glassdoor.note: Glassdoor live: 4.3★ on 2,123 reviews · 80% recommend · 86% CEO approval (Vikram Chopra) · 82% positive business outlook.

attrition.note: Official May 2026 dept-wise attrition report. Overall org attrition = 7.78% · Tech 8.82% · Product 11.85% · Marketing 3.49%. Live attrition (base-live) = 345 exits. Exit-reason mix shown as share of May exits. The recruitment 30-day new-joiner attrition (7.14% overall) is a separate TA metric, reported in Recruitment KPIs — not the org figure.
attrition._source: May 2026 official dept-wise attrition report (Joining Data / base-live · Superset). Exit-reason mix from HRMS exit reasons (May 2026).

snowflake_catalog.source: CSPL_HR_DB · Fivetran-synced from HRMS Public / HR_ATS Public

headline.label: Total Active HC
headline.sub: Cars24 · CAPL merged into CSPL · per Superset

headline.label: Cars24 (CSPL)
headline.sub: Single entity · CAPL now part of CSPL

categories.name: Active Headcount
categories.source: Superset · official HC (CAPL employees migrated into CSPL — CAPL no longer a separate entity)
categories.formula: COUNT(DISTINCT active employees) · single Cars24 entity (CSPL) after CAPL merge.

categories.name: Org Structure
categories.formula: COUNT(*) GROUP BY BAND WHERE EMPLOYEE_STATUS='Active' · Builders = B4 + B5 + B6 + 'Builder' band collapsed (roll-up; sub-lines NOT additive)

categories.name: Recruitment

metrics.label: Reqs · New
metrics.sub: ATS REQUISITIONS · Status=Open

categories.source: Recruitment Data · Rocket Open REQs (May 2026 official)
categories.formula: Open Reqs & Open Positions from Rocket Open REQs (Status=Open). Avg TAT (open) = avg days a requisition has been open. NOTE: joinings/YTJ/drops are taken from the Recruitment sheet, not Snowflake (Snowflake base includes internal movements).

categories.name: Interviews

metrics.label: Panel Size (avg interviewers / slot)
metrics.sub: 1.0 = pure 1-on-1. 1.16 = ~16% of slots have 2+ panelists. Lower = less rigour at senior rounds.

metrics.label: Completion Rate
metrics.sub: 27,921 of 31,652 slots completed. 3,249 still scheduled, 482 rescheduled.

metrics.label: No-Show Rate
metrics.sub: 1,061 of 36,736 feedbacks flagged DID_NOT_HAPPEN (candidate / interviewer no-show).

metrics.label: Reschedule Rate
metrics.sub: 2,876 of 31,652 slots rescheduled ≥1× · 14 slots rescheduled 5+ times (red-flag candidates / loops)

metrics.label: Virtual %
metrics.sub: 29,542 Virtual vs 2,110 Physical. Lever: in-person at B3+ for stronger signal?

metrics.label: Overall Select Rate
metrics.sub: 28,550 YES / (28,550 YES + 2,854 NO) verdicts. Excludes blanks & DID_NOT_HAPPEN.

metrics.label: Round 1 Select Rate
metrics.sub: 14,091 YES / 16,360 decided · This is the screening filter — tightening R1 reduces panel load downstream.

metrics.label: Round 2 Select Rate
metrics.sub: 8,937 YES / 9,380 decided · If 95%+ pass R2, R2 may be redundant for some funcs

metrics.label: Round 3 Select Rate
metrics.sub: 4,126 YES / 4,248 decided · Rounds 3+ are 95%+ pass — final rounds barely filter, mostly confirmatory

metrics.label: Round Funnel: R1→R2→R3→R4→R5
metrics.sub: Biggest drop is R3→R4 (73%). R1→R2 = 51%. Helps identify which round is doing the real filtering by function.

metrics.label: #1 Reject Reason
metrics.sub: 1,359 of 2,854 NO verdicts. TA signal: top JD / candidate-match issue — fix at sourcing level.

metrics.label: #2 Reject Reason
metrics.sub: 573 NOs · Tighten R1 technical screen.

metrics.label: #3 Reject Reason
metrics.sub: 254 NOs

metrics.label: #4 Reject Reason
metrics.sub: 207 NOs (Communication Skills label only). Adding Oral Communication (25), combined comm-related = 232 = 8.1% of all rejects.

metrics.label: Cultural Fit Rejects
metrics.sub: Cultural Fit Issues (166) + Cultural/Team Dynamics (27) combined — small but worth tracking by hiring manager for bias signals

metrics.label: AI Feedback Adoption
metrics.sub: 3,704 of 36,736 feedbacks used AI summary. 89.9% still manual — big lift opportunity to save IC time.

categories.formula: Schedules = COUNT(INTERVIEW_SCHEDULE) · Feedback = COUNT(INTERVIEW_FEEDBACK) · Select Rate = YES / (YES+NO) excluding blanks · Panel Size = Feedback ÷ Schedule

categories.name: Live Attrition

metrics.label: Exits MTD
metrics.sub: May'26 live attrition (base-live)

metrics.label: Exits YTD
metrics.sub: WHAT: Total CSPL employees who left Cars24 since 1 Jan 2026. WHY: Year-to-date attrition volume. ACTION: Divide by 4.5 months & annualize to project full-year exits ≈ 3,960.

metrics.label: Trailing 12-Month Exits
metrics.sub: WHAT: Every CSPL exit in the last 365 days (rolling, not calendar-year). WHY: Smoother view than YTD — doesn't reset every Jan. ACTION: This is the right denominator for benchmarking attrition vs. industry.

metrics.label: Annualized Attrition (rough)
metrics.sub: WHAT: 4,988 trailing-12mo exits ÷ 6,896 current CSPL Active HC = 72.3%. WHY: Headline attrition rate the way most companies report it. ACTION: Auto-retail frontline industry typically sees 40–80% — Cars24 is in band, but high. Drill by function: frontline likely >100%, corporate <20%.

metrics.label: Retention Save Rate
metrics.sub: WHAT: 2,157 employees who resigned but later withdrew their resignation, out of 13,315 decided cases. WHY: Direct measure of how often managers/HR successfully retain a resigning employee. ACTION: Higher = strong ATL (Attempt-to-Lose) conversation culture. <10% would be alarming. 16% is healthy.

metrics.label: Separation Workflow · Lifetime
metrics.sub: WHAT: Total number of resignation/exit workflows ever raised in Cars24 HRMS (since system inception). WHY: Lifetime audit trail. Breaks down to: 6,961 Approved + 3,441 Admin Approved + 2,157 Revoked + 1,722 Pending + 792 Resigned + 756 Rejected. ACTION: Use as denominator for retention/rejection rates.

metrics.label: Pending Approval · Total
metrics.sub: WHAT: Resignation workflows currently sitting at 'Pending with Manager/Approver/Admin' status. WHY: Looks like an HR ops backlog. ACTION: DON'T PANIC — 94% are zombie records >1yr old (see next tile). True backlog is 0.

metrics.label: Pending · ZOMBIES (>1 yr old)
metrics.sub: WHAT: Resignation records stuck 'Pending' for over 365 days. WHY: Employees actually left long ago but the HRMS workflow was never closed. Pure data hygiene rot. ACTION: HR Ops must bulk-close these in HRMS — they inflate every 'pending' metric. Highest age = 1,069 days (~3 years).

metrics.label: Pending · 181–365 days
metrics.sub: WHAT: Pending workflows aged 6 months to 1 year. WHY: Stale but recent enough to investigate. ACTION: HR audit each one — if employee already left, close; if still active, follow up.

metrics.label: Pending · ≤90 days (real backlog)
metrics.sub: WHAT: Resignations from the last 3 months that are still pending approval. WHY: This is the ONLY number that matters for 'is HR keeping up?'. ACTION: Zero = managers and HR are processing exits on time. Operationally clean.

metrics.label: FnF · Processed (Lifetime)
metrics.sub: WHAT: Total Full & Final settlements ever closed in HRMS. Includes Positive F&F (12,153 — clean exit) + Negative F&F (4,783 — money recovered from employee) + others (458). WHY: Lifetime FnF audit. ACTION: Use to benchmark FnF cycle-time and accuracy.

metrics.label: FnF · On Hold (truly pending)
metrics.sub: WHAT: FnFs flagged 'Hold' — disputed amounts, missing clearance, asset recovery pending, etc. WHY: These are the REAL pending FnFs that need HR/Finance action. ACTION: Each one blocks an employee's final payout — should be a weekly ops review item.

metrics.label: Top Exit Reason #1
metrics.sub: WHAT: 25.5% of all reasoned exits (2,649 of 10,369) cite a better external offer. WHY: Comp + growth + brand pull are losing the war for talent. ACTION: TA to track competitor companies poaching us; comp team to benchmark; L&D to surface internal mobility paths.

metrics.label: Top Exit Reason #2
metrics.sub: WHAT: 21.1% of all reasoned exits (2,190 of 10,369) — vague catch-all reason employees pick to avoid a hard conversation. WHY: Likely hides real causes (manager, comp, role). ACTION: Push exit interviews to probe specifics; consider removing 'Personal Issues' as a default option.

metrics.label: Top Exit Reason #3
metrics.sub: WHAT: 15.1% of reasoned exits — another catch-all (almost a duplicate of #2). WHY: Personal Issues + Personal Reasons combined = 36.2% of all exits = 3,757 cases we have NO real reason for. ACTION: HR system fix — merge these labels, force more specific reason picks.

metrics.label: Job Dissatisfaction Exits
metrics.sub: WHAT: 6.6% of reasoned exits (681 of 10,369) — employees who explicitly said they were unhappy with the work itself. WHY: Direct role-design / job-fit signal. ACTION: Drill by department — hotspots reveal teams where the actual job differs from what was sold at hire.

metrics.label: Health-related Exits
metrics.sub: WHAT: 8.4% of reasoned exits (868 of 10,369) — exits cited 'Health Problem'. WHY: Could be wellness, shift patterns, on-job stress (esp. frontline ops). ACTION: Cross with function — if concentrated in field ops, look at shift timings, physical demands, insurance utilisation.

metrics.label: 🚩 Manager-issue Exits
metrics.sub: WHAT: Employees who explicitly cited 'Issues with Manager' as exit reason. WHY: Small in volume but extremely high signal — most employees won't put this in writing. Each one is a flag on a specific manager. ACTION: Pull manager name → if any manager has 2+ such exits, escalate for ATL coaching.

metrics.label: 🚩 Absconding
metrics.sub: WHAT: Employees who left without serving notice or formal resignation. WHY: BGV black mark for future employers; usually FnF-negative (we recover joining bonus / training cost). ACTION: Check if concentrated by location/role — could indicate hiring quality issue at source.

categories.window: MTD = May'26 · live attrition 345 (base-live) · lifetime tiles cumulative · as of 09 Jun 2026
categories.formula: Exits = COUNT(*) on EMPLOYEES.DATE_OF_EXIT in window · Pending Approval = SEPARATIONS.STATUS LIKE 'Pending%' · Pending age = DATEDIFF(today − TRIGGERED_DATE) · FnF Hold = FNF_DATA.FNF_STATUS='Hold' · Save Rate = Revoked ÷ (Approved+AdminApproved+Revoked+Rejected)

categories.name: Employee Type
categories.formula: COUNT(*) GROUP BY EMPLOYEE_TYPE WHERE EMPLOYEE_STATUS='Active'. Total = 6,895 live CSPL active (same base as Bands tab). The 8 employee types shown sum to 6,891; the other 4 are non-standard system records (3 Test Employee + 1 Suspended).

categories.name: Bands · Active HC
categories.formula: COUNT(*) GROUP BY BAND WHERE EMPLOYEE_STATUS='Active' · Builders = B4 + B5 + B6 + 'Builder' band (roll-up; sub-lines NOT additive). 🔴 Serving notice period = Active employees with a resignation in progress (Administrator Approved + Pending with Manager) — a senior-talent flight-risk signal.

metrics.label: B2 · Senior IC
metrics.sub: 🔴 33 serving notice period (3.1%)

metrics.label: B3 · Manager / Lead
metrics.sub: 🔴 16 serving notice period (5.6%)

metrics.label: Builders (B4+B5+B6)
metrics.sub: 🔴 23 serving notice period (10.0%) — highest senior flight risk

metrics.label: FY25 live appraisals

metrics.sub: CSPL — all cycles since FY21

categories.window: FY25 live (PMS FY 2025 Pre-filled + Self Reflection) · FY24 calibrated (PMS_FY23-24_SR) · lifetime for totals · CSPL
categories.formula: COUNT(*) grouped by REVIEW_CYCLE_NAME / CURRENT_STAGE / CALIBRATION_RATING

categories.name: Background Verification

metrics.label: Candidates ever BGV'd
metrics.sub: Unique candidates across 58,406 doc-level checks (~6 docs each)

metrics.label: Fully cleared candidates
metrics.sub: 7% — 212 clean + 433 closed with resolved issues. No pending docs.

metrics.label: Candidates in progress
metrics.sub: 93% have at least one pending doc — 2,169 of these also have a red flag

metrics.label: Stale pending (90d+ old)
metrics.sub: 91% of the 33,426 pending bucket is 90+ days old — likely abandoned, needs cleanup

metrics.label: L3M BGV raised
metrics.sub: Feb–May 2026: 904 completed, 2,921 still pending, 6 issues

metrics.label: L90D real red flags
metrics.sub: issue_highlighted across all recent BGV — discovery rate <0.2%

metrics.label: Lifetime serious red flags
metrics.sub: Discrepancy 525 + On Hold 93 + Case Stopped 67 + Issue Highlighted 30

metrics.label: Lifetime BGV docs
metrics.sub: Pending 33,426 · Completed 21,176 · Insufficient 2,098 · Amber 991 · Other 715

categories.name: HR Assets

metrics.label: Lifetime asset spend
metrics.sub: Sum of ASSET_COST across 28,451 assets — total HR hardware + tools capex since inception

metrics.label: Current book value
metrics.sub: Sum of CURRENT_COST — ~64% has depreciated off the books

metrics.label: Active deployment value
metrics.sub: 12,891 allocated assets (45% of fleet) — what's actually in employees' hands

metrics.label: In-stock dead capital
metrics.sub: 4,170 assets sitting in stock — paid for, not deployed

metrics.label: Lost / short / disputed
metrics.sub: 419 short-found + 243 disputed + 44 lost-under-insurance + 21 lost — ₹1.34 Cr purchase value exposure

metrics.label: Total asset records
metrics.sub: Hardware 18,613 · Tools 9,837 · Uniform 1 — refreshed live

metrics.label: Allocated / In stock / Maintenance
metrics.sub: Active fleet (18,114). Disposed (sold/scrap/exit/written-off) = 9,192. Other (working/moved) = 418.

metrics.label: Data quality flag
metrics.sub: Disputed case casing dupes: 'Disputed case' (180) + 'Disputed Case' (63) — needs merge in source

categories.window: Lifetime — all assets ever recorded · live valuation in INR
categories.formula: COUNT(*) and SUM(ASSET_COST/CURRENT_COST) grouped by STOCK_STATUS / ASSET_TYPE

categories.formula: Present% = COUNT(STATUS LIKE 'P%') ÷ COUNT(STATUS IN P/A/L variants). Excludes Weekly Off (W) and Holiday (H) from denominator.

categories.name: Directories · PII & Diversity

metrics.label: Female % · active workforce
metrics.sub: 1,009 of 6,896 active CSPL employees. Hash-mapped from HRMS GENDER column (5,877 Male / 1,009 Female / 10 Other)

metrics.label: Female % · applicant pool
metrics.sub: 3,256 Female of 20,341 ATS applicants who declared gender — pipeline mix slightly better than internal HC

metrics.sub: 42,302 of 63,659 applicants have blank gender — recruiters not capturing the field consistently

metrics.label: PII records on file

metrics.label: Founder action
metrics.sub: DOB is the standout data gap. Backfill from joining forms / ID docs to unlock age diversity + statutory age reporting

categories.formula: Female% = COUNT(hash=10758599872564880747) ÷ COUNT(Active). PII coverage = COUNT(col IS NOT NULL) ÷ COUNT(*). ATS data gap = COUNT(GENDER='') ÷ COUNT(*).

snowflake_catalog._source: Snowflake CSPL_HR_DB · Fivetran-synced from HRMS Public / HR_ATS Public schemas

_master_source_note: Mixed-source dashboard. Snowflake CSPL_HR_DB is the system of record for headcount, joinings/exits, function breakdown, talent density. Recruitment Metrics CSVs (Joining Master + Rocket Offers + Base-Live) own operational metrics — drops, OTJ, source-of-hire, recruiter productivity, hike%, CPH, gender (HRMS PII hashed). Each subsection has _source.

hrbp._source: P&C HRBP monthly update (workbook narrative tabs) · synced 09 Jun 2026

items.text: Conducted 12 induction sessions across teams in May 2026
items.output: New joiners onboarded with a structured Day-1 experience across departments; faster integration and early engagement.
Improved new-joiner readiness, reduced early-attrition risk, and strengthened leadership connect before Day 1.

items.text: Managed 5 retention / conversion cases: Manmeet Singh (Data Scientist) — retained; Teena (Growth Marketing) — converted to full-time from internship; Prince Popli — retained; Abhishek Khandelwal — offered full-time post-internship; Paridhi Agarwal — retained
items.output: Key talent retained; new-hire costs reduced through internal absorption and conversion.

items.text: Conducted ATL calls (19 cases) to ensure smooth, dignified exits for separated employees
items.output: Smooth exit process ensured; proper documentation and communication handled for all separation cases.

items.text: Organised & executed the Mother's Day event — wishes board in the central area; invited employees and their mothers to the office
items.output: Boosted engagement and workplace culture; strengthened sense of belonging.

items.text: TOKEN '26 Hackathon — participant coordination, follow-ups, and clear process communication
items.output: Seamless event execution; stronger engagement, employer branding, and talent pool.

items.text: Managed May-born birthday celebrations + 5-year & 10-year service-milestone trophy distribution
items.output: Inclusive, appreciative culture; reinforced long-term association and recognition of tenured employees.

items.text: T-shirt distribution + welcome card & 'Welcome to Cars24' desk hoarding for new joiners
items.output: Stronger employer branding; warm, personalised Day-1 experience.

items.text: AI Interview went live on Darwin for Product Manager, Product Designer, HR, SEO Analyst and AI Analytics Engineer roles — structured, scalable screening
items.output: Optimised panellist time; reduced manual interview bandwidth; improved screening quality and process efficiency.
